name: The Muni
address: 1400 14th St NW Suite B, Washington, DC 20005
about: The Muni is a golf simulator venue and bar located in Washington, D.C. The facility features state-of-the-art golf simulators, interactive putting greens, and electronic dartboards, all designed for play without formal golf experience. It operates as an adult-oriented establishment with a laid-back, social atmosphere that blends gaming with a full bar and kitchen service. The venue caters to both casual players and groups seeking a unique social outing, with an emphasis on fun, accessibility, and entertainment over technical golf instruction.
why_golfers_like_it: All-in-one venue combining golf simulation with interactive games like darts and virtual putting
No reservations needed, with a walk-in, first-come-first-served policy for easy access
Customizable bays and games suitable for players of all skill levels

food_and_drink.notes: Full kitchen available with personal pan pizzas and other food items; QR ordering for delivery to your spot
Full bar with craft cocktails, including specialty drinks like the 'John Daly'; no BYOB allowed

faq.question: Do I need my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: No, personal golf clubs are not permitted; clubs are provided at the venue.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: No outside food or drinks are allowed; the venue offers a full menu and bar accessible via QR ordering.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes varies depending on group size and play pace, but typically ranges from 1.5 to 2.5 hours.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: No, The Muni is a bar and strictly for those 21 years and over; no children are permitted.
